verified.notes: Age 60+ qualifies for simplified eligibility rules with no income limit requirement; resource test of $4,500 applies if income exceeds limit (limit amount not specified in sources). Asset limit does not include home or primary vehicle value. No separate couple asset limit found; $4,500 figure applies to households. Phone number is Vermont Foodbank support line for 3SquaresVT assistance as of March 2026.

Phone number is Vermont Foodbank support line for 3SquaresVT assistance as of March 2026.

verified.notes: Vermont has multiple Meals on Wheels programs with different eligibility criteria. Age Well (largest provider) serves age 60+. VCIL serves under-60 individuals with chronic conditions or disabilities. No income or asset limits found in search results for either program. Phone number is Age Well's main helpline. Note: Program eligibility varies by provider and region; no single unified state program found.

programs.notes: Vermont has multiple Meals on Wheels programs with different eligibility criteria. Age Well (largest provider) serves age 60+. VCIL serves under-60 individuals with chronic conditions or disabilities. No income or asset limits found in search results for either program. Phone number is Age Well's main helpline. Note: Program eligibility varies by provider and region; no single unified state program found.

verified.notes: Age requirement (55+) confirmed from U.S. Department of Labor official source. Official phone number for Vermont SCSEP not provided in search results; contact A4TD directly or Vermont state workforce agency for current contact information.
